results.title: Sendr: Your complete GTM stack, powered by AI | Product Hunt
results.content:
Multi-channel sequences across Email, LinkedIn (live), WhatsApp (soon)

Personalised video and voice at scale, in sequence

Native video in DMs that play directly in inbox

SendrAI to enrich, score and adapt the sequence based on engagement

Behaviour-based automation across the entire sequence

1

41

Sendr
Dave Cannell

4mo ago

### SendrAI - Agentic Data Enrichment - LIVE!

We re excited to announce that SendrAI 2.0 has just landed, rebuilt from the ground up to make outreach faster, smarter and easier than ever.  
Use SendrAI to immediately find live signals, research, lead scoring and enrichments - from simple natural language input.

Check out a glimpse of SendrAI in action here:

4

That is one of the best GTM tools available at the moment. The idea is unique and valuable. As an early-stage beta-tester, we were able to increase our pipeline conversions almost x2, and we see some other ways to improve it even more!

#### vs Alternatives

Because this tool is highly effective, it solves my business pain points, and it was easy to integrate Sendr into our processes

#### What's great

Now I’m creating super-personalized experiences for my clients, including amazing dedicated landing pages — all thanks to Sendr! It works incredibly well!

The platform keeps improving day by day, and the team behind it is doing an outstanding job. Their support is fast, friendly, and always available.

results.title: Giselle: Build and run AI workflows. Open source. | Product Hunt
results.content:
That journey shaped what Giselle is today: an AI app builder optimized for product ops and GitHub-native workflows.

What makes Giselle different:

GitHub as your vector store — Turn your repos, issues, PRs, and code into RAG-ready context with one click. No pipeline setup.

Event-driven workflows — Trigger Giselle apps from GitHub events (new issue, PR comment, etc.). Build your own CodeRabbit-style review agent — no code required.

Team-first, cloud-native — Apps you build are instantly shareable. Call them from a chat UI ("Stage") or directly from GitHub with custom slash commands (you define the command name).

What you can build:

✅ Automated PR review agents

✅ PRD drafters that pull context from your codebase

✅ Spec/docs updaters triggered by merged PRs [...] The tutorial walks through building an issue assistant that automatically researches context and posts helpful comments when new issues are created. Set up takes about 15 minutes, no code required.

Got questions about event-driven automation? Drop them in the comments.

Takafumi Endo
Giselle

Giselle

Taking it further! Yesterday's issue assistant was just the warm-up—now we're building something teams actually ask for: a custom PR review agent.

Building Your Own PR Review Agent with Giselle  

CodeRabbit and Copilot are great, but every team has different needs. This tutorial shows how to build a review agent that understands your codebase using Agentic RAG—so feedback is grounded in your actual code, not generic best practices.

Kaori Nakashima
Giselle

Giselle [...] And if you know of better products out there, please let us know! We're always looking to learn from great tools.

Anna R

@codenote Huge congrats. Upvoted!   
  
This is a breath of fresh air for the AI automation space! I completely relate to that feeling of "whack-a-mole" when trying to debug a complex LLM chain—it’s usually so opaque that you have no idea where the logic broke.

The visual node editor is the real hero here. Being able to mix and match models from different providers on a single canvas is exactly the kind of flexibility power users need.

Question for the team: How does the real-time tracking handle high-concurrency workflows? Is there a specific "debug mode" that lets you inspect the exact prompt/response at each node?

🚀

Tadashi Shigeoka
Giselle

Giselle
